cot_prefix: Okay, let's read the passage and figure out the correct order of the places from east to west. The question is asking for the correct order based on the passage. From the passage, we know that the businesses are lined up from San Francisco to New York. So San Francisco must be to the west and New York to the east. The line of flagpoles starts in San Francisco and goes to New York. The passage says: "From San Francisco to about Reno, it is almost unnoticeable..." This suggests that Reno is east of San Francisco. Then it says, "From Reno eastward the poles increase in height until, near Columbus, Ohio..." So Columbus is east of Reno. Finally, the passage mentions approaching the eastern terminus (New York), so New York is the easternmost point. So the order from west to east is: San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, New York. But the question is asking for the order from east to west. That would be the reverse of the order I just determined. So from east to west, it's New York, Columbus, Reno, San Francisco. Looking at the answer choices: (A) San Francisco, Reno, New York, Columbus. - This is not correct because New York is east of Columbus. (B) San Francisco, Reno, Columbus, New York. - This is from west to east.
